name: Arctic Wolf
description: Monitor Arctic Wolf managed detection and response (MDR) platform for security ticket management, incident tracking, and threat response compliance

config.setupInstructions:
1. Sign in to the Arctic Wolf Unified Portal at https://dashboard.arcticwolf.com
2. Go to Organization Profile > Personal API Keys
3. Click Create an API Key, give it a name, and select an expiry period
4. Copy the API key token (you will not be able to view it again)
5. Find your POD and Organization UUID by calling the Organizations API:
   curl https://eloc.global-prod.arcticwolf.net/api/v1/organizations -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
6. Note the 'pod' value (e.g., us001) and the 'id' value (your Organization UUID) from the response

credentialFields.label: Personal API Key
credentialFields.helpText: Generate in Arctic Wolf Unified Portal: Organization Profile > Personal API Keys > Create an API Key

credentialFields.label: POD (Region)
credentialFields.helpText: Your Arctic Wolf POD name (e.g., us001, us002). Find it via the Organizations API or ask your Concierge Security Team.

credentialFields.label: Organization UUID
credentialFields.helpText: Your Arctic Wolf organization UUID. Find it via the Organizations API or in the Unified Portal.

checks.name: API Connectivity
checks.description: Verifies connectivity to the Arctic Wolf API and that the Personal API Key is valid by retrieving the organization list

checks.name: Incident Response Monitoring
checks.description: Checks for open and recent incident tickets in Arctic Wolf to verify active incident response monitoring

checks.name: Ticket Management
checks.description: Reviews all Arctic Wolf security tickets to ensure active ticket management and timely resolution

checks.name: Threat Detection Coverage
checks.description: Verifies Arctic Wolf MDR is actively monitoring by checking for recent tickets that indicate threat detection activity
